# StatistikkbankR

An R-package for accessing Statistikkbank data from Statistics Norway
(SSB).

Statistics Norway (SSB) is Norway’s central statistical authority. It
publishes hundreds of official statistics tables through its
Statistikkbank service, covering topics such as population, economy,
labour, housing, and health.

The `StatistikkbankR` package gives you a direct R interface to the
Statistikkbank API. You can search the table catalogue, inspect table
structure, and download data as tidy `data.frame` or `tibble` objects,
directly in R.

This package has been written using GitHub Copilot with Codex and
Claude.

## Installation

Install the development version from GitHub:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("navikt/statistikkbankR")
```

### Nav firewall

If you work in Nav and use VDI Analyse, R may fail to verify SSL
certificates and you get an error when trying to install. In that case,
try running the command:

``` r
Sys.setenv(R_LIBCURL_SSL_REVOKE_BEST_EFFORT = "TRUE")
```

## Finding a table

Use
[`ssb_search()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_search.md)
to find tables by keyword. The function returns a data frame with one
row per matching table; the `id` column is what you will pass to other
functions.

``` r
library(StatistikkbankR)

# Search for tables about population (in Norwegian)
results <- ssb_search("befolkning")
results[, c("id", "label", "updated")]

# Or search in English
ssb_search("population", language = "en")
```

SSB paginates search results; use `fetch_all = TRUE` if you want all
pages at once.

``` r
# Retrieve all pages at once
all_results <- ssb_search("arbeid", fetch_all = TRUE)
nrow(all_results)
```

Alternatively, you can find tables of interest on SSB’s
[website](https://www.ssb.no/statbank).

## Inspecting a table before downloading

Once you have a table identifier, use
[`ssb_describe()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_describe.md)
to see its structure before downloading it. This is the recommended
first command to run once you have a table you’re interested in.

``` r
desc <- ssb_describe("07459")

# Table-level summary: title, last updated, estimated total rows
desc$table

# One row per dimension: id, label, role, number of codes
desc$dimensions
```

Use
[`ssb_codes()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codes.md)
to list the valid values you can use as filters for a given dimension:

``` r
# All valid codes for the Region dimension
ssb_codes("07459", "Region")

# All valid time periods
ssb_codes("07459", "Tid")
```

## Downloading data

### Without filters

Pass a table identifier to
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
to download the full table. SSB enforces a cell limit on large tables;
the function will warn you and stop if the estimated row count is too
high.

``` r
# Download all data (only works for small tables)
data <- get_ssb_data("05803")
head(data)
```

### Filtering by dimension

Pass dimension identifiers as named arguments to select only the rows
you need. Each argument accepts a character vector of codes.

``` r
# Download population for specific regions for the years 2020, 2021 and 2022
pop <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    Region = c("0301", "1103"),
    Tid    = 2020:2022
)
pop
```

### Server-side expressions

SSB supports a set of shorthand expressions that are evaluated on the
server before data is returned. You can use these as filter values:

| Expression    | Meaning                                          |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `"top(n)"`    | The n most recent time periods                   |
| `"bottom(n)"` | The n oldest time periods                        |
| `"all(*)"`    | All codes (the default if you omit the argument) |

``` r
# The three most recent years for all regions
pop_recent <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    Tid = "top(3)"
)
```

For more details on the possible query expressions, see the [SSB API
documentation](https://www.ssb.no/en/api/pxwebapiv2).

## Long format and singleton dimensions

By default
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
returns data in wide format: one column per measure code. Use
`table_format = "long"` to get a long table instead, with one row per
observation.

``` r
pop_long <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    Region = c("0301", "1103"),
    Tid    = 2020:2022,
    table_format = "long"
)
head(pop_long)
```

When filtering to a single code in one dimension, that dimension
collapses to a constant and is dropped from the wide output but retained
in long output by default. Pass `include_singleton_dims = TRUE` with
`table_format = "long"` to include those constant-value columns
explicitly:

``` r
pop_one_region <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    Region                 = "0301",
    Tid                    = "top(3)",
    table_format           = "long",
    include_singleton_dims = TRUE
)
```

## Time classes (zoo::yearqtr and zoo::yearmon)

By default,
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
converts quarterly time codes to
[`zoo::yearqtr`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/zoo/man/yearqtr.html) and monthly
time codes to
[`zoo::yearmon`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/zoo/man/yearmon.html).

``` r
# Quarterly table (12452): Tid becomes yearqtr by default
q_dat <- get_ssb_data(
    "12452",
    Tid = "top(4)",
    character_as_factor = FALSE
)
class(q_dat$Tid_label)

# Disable quarter conversion and keep raw SSB codes like "2024K1"
q_raw <- get_ssb_data(
    "12452",
    Tid = "top(4)",
    character_as_factor = FALSE,
    convert_quarter_to_yearqtr = FALSE
)
head(q_raw$Tid_label)

# Monthly table (13966): Tid becomes yearmon by default
m_dat <- get_ssb_data(
    "13966",
    Tid = "top(4)",
    character_as_factor = FALSE
)
class(m_dat$Tid_label)

# Disable month conversion and keep raw SSB codes like "2024M01"
m_raw <- get_ssb_data(
    "13966",
    Tid = "top(4)",
    character_as_factor = FALSE,
    convert_month_to_yearmon = FALSE
)
head(m_raw$Tid_label)
```

## Codelists and geographic boundary changes

SSB provides aggregation codelists that let you request data at a
different grouping level — for example, historical municipality
boundaries — without manually remapping individual codes.

SSB’s [`klassR`](https://statisticsnorway.github.io/ssb-klassr/) package
provides an easy interface for retrieving SSB classifications, and may
be used alongside this package.

Use
[`ssb_codelists()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codelists.md)
to discover which codelists are available:

``` r
ssb_codelists("07459")

# Filter to the geographic dimension
ssb_codelists("07459", dimension = "Region")
```

Use
[`ssb_codelist_details()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codelist_details.md)
to inspect the group-to-member mapping inside a specific codelist:

``` r
cl <- ssb_codelist_details("agg_KommSummer")
cl$codelist   # metadata
cl$values     # group codes and their members
```

Apply a codelist when downloading data by passing the `codelists` and
`output_values` arguments to
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md):

``` r
pop_agg <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    Tid           = 2020:2022,
    codelists     = list(Region = "agg_KommSummer"),
    output_values = list(Region = "aggregated")
)
```

Or use the convenience wrapper
[`ssb_get_by_codelist()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_get_by_codelist.md)
which identifies the correct dimension automatically:

``` r
pop_agg <- ssb_get_by_codelist(
    "07459",
    "agg_KommSummer",
    Tid          = 2020:2022,
    output_value = "aggregated"
)
```

To join the aggregated result back to individual municipality codes, use
[`ssb_expand_codelist_mapping()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_expand_codelist_mapping.md):

``` r
pop_expanded <- ssb_expand_codelist_mapping(
    data        = pop_agg,
    code_col    = "Region_code",
    codelist_id = "agg_KommSummer"
)
head(pop_expanded)
```

## Performance tips

### Caching

By default,
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
and all helper functions cache table metadata in memory for the duration
of your R session. Set `cache = FALSE` to disable caching, or
`refresh_metadata = TRUE` to discard the cached copy and fetch fresh
metadata:

``` r
# Force a fresh metadata fetch (e.g. after SSB updates the table)
get_ssb_data("07459", Tid = "top(1)", refresh_metadata = TRUE)
```

For details on when SSB updates their tables, see their
[https://www.ssb.no/en/statbank/](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/website).

### Large tables

SSB imposes a cell limit on queries.
[`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
estimates the result size before sending the request and stops with a
message if the query is too large. To proceed anyway, set
`override_large_query = TRUE`:

``` r
data <- get_ssb_data(
    "07459",
    override_large_query = TRUE
)
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`get_ssb_data()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/get_ssb_data.md)
  : Download data from Statistics Norway (SSB)
- [`ssb_codelist_details()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codelist_details.md)
  : Inspect the members of an SSB codelist
- [`ssb_codelists()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codelists.md)
  : List available codelists for an SSB table
- [`ssb_codes()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_codes.md)
  : List valid codes for one dimension of an SSB table
- [`ssb_describe()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_describe.md)
  : Describe an SSB table and its dimensions
- [`ssb_expand_codelist_mapping()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_expand_codelist_mapping.md)
  : Expand grouped codelist codes to individual member codes
- [`ssb_get_by_codelist()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_get_by_codelist.md)
  : Download SSB data using a codelist without specifying the dimension
- [`ssb_search()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_search.md)
  : Search SSB tables by keyword
- [`ssb_units()`](https://navikt.github.io/StatistikkbankR/reference/ssb_units.md)
  : List measurement units for an SSB table
